rulings.Case Title: AARON ANDERSON VS. 11TH & FOLSOM PRESERVATION AND COMMUNITY CENTERS, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Re: Dismissal Of The Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 2, 2010, line 1, Hearing Re: Dismissal Of The Cross-Complaint. Matter continued to May 17, 2010 for hearing. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND VS. ONVOI BUSINESS SOLUTIONS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 2, 2010, line 10, DEFENDANT SELECT PERSONNEL SERVICES, INC DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Continued to March 10, 2010 per agreement of parties. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD CHUN VS. VICTOR CHEW CHUN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Order Awarding Costs Dand Attys Fees Incurred In Partition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 2, 2010, line 9, PLAINTIFF RONALD CHUN Motion For Order Awarding Costs and Attorney Fees Incurred In Partition. Ronald Chun's attorney fees and costs incurred for the common benefit total $32,790.50. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD CHUN VS. VICTOR CHEW CHUN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reimbursement Of Expenses Of Maintaining Property
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 2, 2010, line 9, PLAINTIFF RONALD CHUN Motion For Reimbursement of Expenses of Maintaining Property. Ronald Chun is reimbursed $3,450 from the retained income generated from the "Pre Death Properties." The expense is a normal and reasonable cost of maintaining the properties. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD CHUN VS. VICTOR CHEW CHUN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition For Order Determining: (1) Reimbursement Of Attorneys' Fees Incurred In Partition Action And To Collect Loans, (2) Reasonable Amount Of Attorney Fees And Costs Incurred By Herbert Chew To Enforce Notes Secured By Real Property Partitioned By Court Order In This Actions, And (3) Appropriate Allocation Of Fee Payment To Herbert From Among The Co-Obligors [Ccp 847.101 Et Seq.]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 2, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANTS HERBERT CHEW, and, LOIS CHEW Petition For Order Determining: (1) Reimbursement Of Attorneys' Fees Incurred In Partition Action and To Collect Loans, (2) Reasonable Amount Of Attorney Fees and Costs Incurred By Herbert Chew To Enforce Notes Secured By Real Property Partitioned By Court Order In This Actions, and (3) Appropriate Allocation of Fee Payment To Herbert From Among The Co-Obligors [CCP 847.101 et seq.]. Hearing required. Petitioner Chew to produce copy of agreement that provides court may determine reasonable fee award. Regarding partition fees, court cannot determine which incurred for the common benefit. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD CHUN VS. VICTOR CHEW CHUN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Costs Of Partition And For Equitable Apportionment Of Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 2, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANTS VICTOR CHUN, MONICA CHUN, WELLINGTON CHUN, ANNA CHUN Motion for Award of Costs of Partition and for Equitable Apportionment of Attorney's Fees. Victor and Wellington Chun's attorney fees and costs incurred for the common benefit total $32,282. Court intends to adopt, proportion of each party's interest: Victor 30.33 %, Wellington 30.33%., Ronald 30.33 % and Herbert 9.00% for each motion as it relates to partition cost allocation. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CHATEAU DE LOUIS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. EVEREST INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 2, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT EVEREST INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication denied. Triable issue of fact raised whether the damages of the type and nature covered by the basic insuring agreement were part of the judgment in the underlying case. (Schroter declaration and Repairs Cost Report.) Triable issue of fact whether defendant's actions amounted to bad faith or were objectively reasonable. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CHATEAU DE LOUIS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. EVEREST INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 2, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT EVEREST INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY Motion To Bifurcate. Motion to bifurcate denied without prejudice to be raised before the trial judge. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: B&B QUALITY FOOD PROVIDERS VS. ALLIED MEAT SERVICE, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 2, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANTS ALLIED MEAT SERVICE, INC, ANTHONY NIGRO MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Motion granted. Evidentiary objections overruled. Plaintiff's counsel awarded $6,465 in attorney fees for those activities and expenses reasonably related to default. Request for delay in lifting the default and default judgment denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE C MCWHIRTER VS. STEVEN A FABBRO
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 2, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT STEVEN FABBRO DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREAS AMUNDIN VS. METHOD360, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPOPRATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 2, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT METHOD360, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment denied, failure to shift burden of proof. Original complaint filed by BTG against plaintiff implicates defendant, alleging unfair competition and the taking away of BTG business. The complaint concerns actions taken by plaintiff while employed by defendant. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SIXTH STREET BALDWIN HOUSE LLC VS. SHERINE HARRISON et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 2, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT SHERINE HARRISON MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is granted, with five days leave to amend. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: U.S. BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS TRUSTEE FOR VS. G. MARGO MIDDLEBROOKS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 2, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANT G. MIDDLEBROOKS MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted, five days leave to amend. Weekend days are deemed "holidays." The notice did not expire until the end of the day on December 21st. (See Lamanna vs. Vogner (1993) 17 Cal.App. 4th Supp. 4.) The holding over begain Decmeber 22nd and prayer must be amended. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ATIQUE REHMAN VS. LARRY WILLIAMSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 2, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANTS LARRY WILLIAMSON, SIBBIE WILLIAMSON Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment. Motion granted. Defendants have moved for relief within a reasonble time and provided a justifiable excuse. The law favors adjudication on the merits. =(302/CWW)
